input: A single weather data point displaying current temperature, humidity, wind speed, and conditions. Read-only display that refreshes periodically. Public data, no login needed.
A file format converter — paste text in one format, get it converted to another. Markdown to HTML, JSON to YAML, CSV to JSON. Stateless tool, no data saved between uses. Anyone can use it.
Display a single digital artwork with title, artist name, medium, and dimensions. Just a read-only showcase card. Visitors can view it upon request.
A single product card for a farm stand showing item name, price per pound, weight available, and farm of origin. Public-facing, visitors browse with consent.

My private wine collection — bottles organized by varietal and region into categories. Sort by vintage year, filter by type (red, white, sparkling). Track when I add and consume bottles. Nobody else sees this data.
A birdwatching field log — an ordered list of sightings by date. Each entry has species, location, and photo. Filter by species. Track sightings over time. Private field notes, just for me.
Vocabulary flashcards for language learning — an ordered deck of word-and-definition pairs. Sort cards by difficulty level. Private study tool, no sharing.
A daily mood check-in tool — rate my mood on a scale, add a journal note. Visualize mood trends as a line chart over weeks and months. Completely private health data.
My personal recipe collection — recipes grouped by cuisine type (Italian, Mexican, Thai). Search and filter by ingredient or cuisine. Sort by prep time. Private cookbook, just for me.
A workout tracker — log exercises, sets, and reps for each gym session. Form-based entry for today's workout. Chart my strength progress over time. Keep it private.

An ordered list of movies I want to watch, queued by priority. Mark each as want-to-watch, watching, or watched to track progress. Sort by genre or date added. Filter by watch status. Private entertainment list.
A color mixing tool — select two colors and see the blend result with RGB and HSL values. Shows complementary and analogous palettes. No data saved between sessions. Anyone can use it.

Track monthly income and expenses across categories — dining, transport, bills, entertainment. Show spending trends as bar charts over time. Filter by category, sort by amount. All data stays on my device, completely private.
Track my houseplants — an ordered list of plants with watering schedule, last-watered date, and growth notes. Sort by next watering date. Filter by room location. Private gardening journal.

A personal health overview combining step count, sleep quality, and heart rate data in one grouped view. Charts for weekly trends. Filter by metric type. I might share specific metrics with my doctor, so consent before sharing.
An interactive city map with markers for restaurants, parks, and landmarks organized in categories. Filter markers by category. Public information, anyone can view without signing up.

A personal calendar with events, recurring appointments, and day, week, and month views. Track deadlines and appointments over time. Filter events by type (work, personal, health). Private schedule, no sharing.

A shared expense tracker for roommates — log who paid what, split bills fairly. Expenses organized by person and category as a grouped collection. Filter entries by person, sort by amount. All roommates must join before they can see or add entries.

A real-time chat room for our study group — chronological message stream where members post messages and share file links. New members need to be added before they can participate. No threading, just a time-ordered message flow.

A two-person collaborative drawing canvas — we take turns adding to each other's sketch. Turn-based creative tool: one person draws, then passes to the other. Private creative space just between the two of us.
